content:
Long time no see, everyone. I'm checking Twitter, prompted followers for a 'five by five' confirmation if the
chart, audio, and volume are good. Thank you for the confirmation.

I need to be careful with the volume, it looks a little too loud. I'll talk as I normally do. We have a few things
to cover this morning. For those new to my live streams, I usually look at live price action and deliver
teaching and review. This morning, I don't anticipate a lot of time for the teaching part, it will depend on the
opening and if we can look at some live price action. No promises, but we'll see what we see.

It's important to keep up with the ICT Mentorship 2023 playlist. Anything I put up, whether a live stream or
lecture, is added to this playlist on my YouTube channel.
For new members, some short housekeeping:
- My advice is to go into the 2022 ICT Mentorship playlist, which has 41 videos.
- The first video sets the tone for what you should be expecting, though you don't strictly need to watch it
for completeness sake.

If you've never seen my content, don't know how to read price action, and want to get down to brass tacks,
I am long-winded because I want to give you value. When I teach, I aim for the highest form of
understanding. If you have a short attention span or aren't interested in knowing everything about incurring
risk, you're setting yourself up for failure.
An educator's passion for education is crucial because your results are a direct reflection of my ability to
communicate the logic. I want to convey as much detail as possible, not just static screenshots of buy/sell
signals. Price action isn't static; it has subtle variances, and understanding these is important, especially for
newcomers.

Many refer to price action through a retail perspective. We don't trade or follow retail logic, nor do we look
at things that permeate this industry through collective books, courses, and regurgitated information. This
is why my content can sometimes seem overwhelming or complicated, as you might be trying to fit what
you think you know into my teachings.
My challenge has always been: don't just believe what I say, go into your charts and see if what I'm saying
is there or not. I'm confident that if you do the due diligence, you will find it.

section_title: THE EFFICACY OF THE 2022 MENTORSHIP MODEL
content:
If you're new and looking for a study program, the best and shortest cut, right to the point, is the 2022
playlist. This model was designed for my daughter, streamlined and time-based, to remove ambiguity about
setup timing or session trading.
The pattern consists of looking for:
- A run on liquidity.
- A shift in market structure.
- Going into a fair value Gap.
This model can be traded in:
- Forex
- Futures contracts (index futures, currency futures)
Note: I don't trade crypto or synthetic instruments (like volatility index), but I have students who swear by
their results in crypto.
This model allows you to go directly into the marketplace and start studying. Many times, when students
share their examples and results on Twitter, you're seeing this very model form. Based on feedback, many
funded companies state that the majority of their profitable payouts are coming from my student base. The
2022 model has bridged the gap between not knowing what they're doing and finding profitability and
consistency.

I have charter members who learned this model one-on-one; every day I would explain what to expect in
price action, and my accuracy has been in the high 90s for years, witnessed by thousands of people, and
now hundreds of thousands. That model can be used on intraday charts and higher time frame charts.
There have been requests to move away from lower intraday charts, as many cannot or do not want to
trade one and five-minute charts. The only reason I focus on shorter time frames in teaching is because it
affords many examples, shows consistency in logic, and helps find continuity in analysis. This leads to
routine, business-like results similar to a job where you do the same monotonous tasks daily. You want
trading to be boring and monotonous.

section_title: A WORD ON TRADING WITH LIVE CAPITAL & DEDICATION
content:
The 2022 model is the best way I can communicate trading simply, and it's working for others. Even those
who started following me last year have been funded, are getting payouts, and doing well; some are even
trading their actual live money.
In my personal opinion, you should not be trading in your first year. There's much to learn about yourself
and price action that cannot be appreciated by just watching a couple of videos and risking real money, or
failing a prop firm challenge. Go slow.
Those doing very well, including six-figure earners (e.g., one did $300,000 in three months, another half a
million), put a lot of work into this beforehand. This is real money, not demo. Many are smashing it in their
own accounts, not just prop-funded ones.

Take your time. Nothing's going to change; the algorithm won't change just because ICT has shared
knowledge. Markets have always behaved this way. In the future, it will only become more efficient, with
less choppy consolidation due to more people involved and thus increased liquidity.
More people in our community using my content is not a disadvantage or concern; it will literally make more
liquidity for efficiency. We capitalize on the retail mindset, not being the 'buy stock, read, then reversal' type.
My approach to trading is that we 'Harvest liquidity,' we hunt it; it's our prey.
Retail logic, like moving average crossovers, has a place and can make money, but predominantly, there's
a large degree of neophytes entering the marketplace. It's important to understand your expectations for
learning.

Retail-minded traders often think that every time an overbought or oversold oscillator goes oversold, the
market should go up. This is not true. I learned this lesson the hard way in my first year and a half, insisting
on buying oversold conditions all the time. I didn't understand bear markets; price can stay oversold for a
long time, leading to buying 'falling daggers,' which is very painful to catch.

In my career, I've tried to communicate things you wouldn't learn elsewhere and translate them into a
language conveyed through charts. The things that make these price moves run aren't always directly in
the charts, which sounds impossible. There are things occurring outside the 'kill zones' and specific traits,
but bridging that knowledge to a constantly repeating chart setup is challenging.
The things I teach, like specific PD Arrays, are 'multipliers.' When looking for a setup, your multiplier is the
thing you use to get into a trade. This isn't the model itself, but the specific entry trigger.
Examples of specific multipliers:
- My order block
- Fair value Gap (inversion or standard)
- Institutional Oracle entry drill
- Optimal trade entry
- Propulsion block
These are specific multipliers, things you're looking for in price action. I teach them, I believe in Month 4 of
the core content (the PD Array Matrix), as they are the easiest for me to communicate using a time-based
chart.

section_title: THE POWER OF VISUAL CONFIRMATION & BACKTESTING
content:
I have other PD Arrays that don't require a chart, but they're so complicated, and some already find what
I've taught complicated. I could know what the market will do at a specific time without a chart, as it's
algorithmic. However, you are here to learn something that repeats over and over, something you can
visually see and trust.
The 2022 model, I promise you, if you study it and spend time with it, engaging in practice sessions and
backtesting (going back and looking at past chart moves), it builds the 'experience space' you require to
lean on.

I've had many requests for longer-term swing trades. In the core content, I teach 'quarterly shifts,' which are
major intermediate-term price swings that take place on daily and weekly charts every two to three months.
This provides a macro perspective.
When I seemingly 'ramble' in these videos or presentations, if you feel I'm not getting to the point, the point
is that I'm teaching you how to navigate these things.

One of the things I discuss in the realm of psychology is that if you're under a lot of stress, not feeling well,
or have experienced a loss in your family, it's important not to trade. Stay away from the charts because
you will be hurting. That pain, discomfort, or even boredom can lead to impulsive actions.
I've recently been going through something emotionally with a pet, which is why I couldn't focus and spent
time away from the charts. She's improving, thank you. But I can't focus when it's like that. Many of you
communicate similar situations in other facets of your life on Twitter breakups, illness, job loss and then
wonder why you're not seeing success.
